Thought 2: Both suspects have motive, means, and opportunity. Kimberly has a clear motive (protecting family honor from Jamal's threat to reveal a secret) and possesses the exact murder weapon (the frog poison) with knowledge of how to use it. She was also at the casino (the crime scene) on the night of the murder. Lillian also has a motive (wanting to inherit Jamal's position) and has the means (access to lethal frog toxins through her profession), but her opportunity is less direct—neighbors saw her sneaking out at night, but there's no specific mention of her being at the casino. However, Kimberly's admission about being at the casino and her direct threat toward Jamal ("I would do anything...") is very damning. Also, Kimberly offered Winston a gin and tonic, which was Jamal's favorite drink—this could be a subtle hint or taunt. Lillian's sneaking out might be unrelated to the murder. Kimberly seems more directly implicated.
